preamble: The transition from a glottal stop to the vowel [a] is most famously represented by the adaptation of the Phoenician letter Aleph (a glottal stop) into the Greek Alpha (the vowel [a]).

examples.note: When Greeks borrowed the Phoenician alphabet, the Aleph (a consonant letter for glottal stop) was repurposed as the vowel Alpha [a], since Greek lacked distinctive glottal stop phonemes.
The PIE laryngeal *h₂, sometimes reconstructed as a pharyngeal or glottal fricative, colors adjacent *e-vowels to [a]. E.g., *h₂ep- (water) → Greek apo-.
